task: Implemented multi-session file locking and session registry to enable concurrent Claude Code sessions with safe shared state management across parallel developers
discussion_summary: Session verified that the multi-session architecture (commit e2f7cb5) successfully landed file-level locking and session registry infrastructure. The system now tracks active Claude PIDs, implements flock-based write locks for .md files, and enables cross-session awareness via session_registry.jsonl. Handover JSON isolation per developer remains intact. Two new goals were added to track remaining work: session registry heartbeat logic and flock-based lock refinement for edge cases.

accomplishments.what: Verified multi-session file locking implementation (e2f7cb5) with flock and session registry infrastructure
Confirmed session-scoped edit cursor prevents parallel session file corruption (commit 3ef2974)
Validated multi-PID registry daemon kills all parallel sessions cleanly (commit 320e652)
Reviewed project-state handover mechanism for parallel session composition (commit 8e01e8a)

next_actions.action: Implement heartbeat logic in session_registry.jsonl: add last_heartbeat timestamp, stale session detection (>5min), and cleanup of dead sessions on startup
next_actions.why: Prevent zombie sessions from holding locks indefinitely; enable safe lock release when a Claude instance crashes

next_actions.action: Stress-test flock-based write lock under high concurrency (3+ simultaneous Claude sessions writing to goals.jsonl, decisions.jsonl, blockers.jsonl)
next_actions.why: Verify lock acquisition/release doesn't deadlock, timeout correctly, or lose writes under realistic team load

next_actions.action: Add lock timeout and retry logic: if flock acquisition exceeds 2s, log warning and queue write; if retry exhausted, escalate to user
next_actions.why: Prevent Claude from hanging indefinitely waiting for locks held by slow or stuck sessions

next_actions.action: Document multi-session concurrency model in README: explain flock strategy, session registry heartbeat, lock timeouts, and how to debug lock contention
next_actions.why: Clarify for users (Bippin, co-founder, Lochan) how askr handles parallel Claude sessions and what to expect

next_actions.action: Add session-aware context injection to session_start.py: load active session registry and surface 'Session X (dev_name) is working on file Y' in Claude's initial context
next_actions.why: Solve shared memory problem — Claude needs to know what other sessions are doing before starting work

decisions.decision: Per-dev handover JSON files (handover_<dev>.json) are isolated by developer, not by session
decisions.reason: Allows one developer to run multiple Claude sessions without file path collisions, but requires session registry for cross-session awareness

decisions.decision: JSONL append-only format is safe for concurrent writes; .md files require flock-based locking
decisions.reason: JSONL union-merge semantics survive concurrent appends; markdown files need explicit locking or serialization

decisions.decision: Use flock (file-level locking) for .md and .jsonl writes rather than distributed locks or git branch conventions
decisions.reason: flock is simple, OS-native, works across parallel processes on same machine, and avoids git merge complexity

decisions.decision: Session registry tracks active Claude PIDs, developer name, start time, and last heartbeat timestamp
decisions.reason: Enables detection of stale/zombie sessions and safe lock cleanup; provides cross-session visibility

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Restricting each developer to a single Claude Code session to avoid race conditions
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: assuming a developer using claude code is restricted to a singular session is basically being optimistic to build a underwhelming system

failed_approaches.approach: Assuming per-dev handover JSON isolation is sufficient for multi-session safety
failed_approaches.reason: Handover JSONs are isolated, but shared .md files (goals.md, decisions.md, blockers.md) require explicit locking to prevent last-write-wins data loss

relational_files.why: Manages session PID tracking, heartbeat, and stale session detection
Loads session registry on startup and surfaces active sessions to Claude context
Acquires flock before writing to shared .md and .jsonl files
Implements flock-based write lock acquisition and release for concurrent file access
